author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-14. Saint Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of St. John and the Epistle to the Hebrews

title: John 13.1

source.title: NPNF1-14. Saint Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of St. John and the Epistle to the Hebrews

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Now before the feast of the Passover, when Jesus knew that His hour was come that He should depart out of this world unto the Father, having loved His own which were in the world, He loved them unto the end.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Be ye imitators of me,” said Paul, “as I also am of Christ.” ( 1 Cor. xi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For on this account He took also flesh of our substance, that by means of it He might teach us virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For (“God sending His own Son) in the likeness of sinful flesh,” it saith, “and for sin condemned sin in the flesh.” ( Rom. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And Christ Himself saith, “Learn of Me, for I am meek and lowly in heart.” ( Matt. xi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this He taught, not by words alone, but by actions also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For they called Him a Samaritan, and one that had a devil, and a deceiver, and cast stones at Him; and at one time the Pharisees sent servants to take Him, at another they sent plotters against Him; and they continued also insulting Him themselves, and that when they had no fault to find, but were even being continually benefited.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Still after such conduct He ceaseth not to do well to them both by words and deeds.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And, when a certain domestic smote Him on the face, He said, “If I have spoken evil, bear witness of the evil, but if well, why smitest thou Me?” ( c. xviii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But this was to those who hated and plotted against Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us see also what He doeth now towards the disciples, or rather what actions He now exhibiteth towards the traitor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The man whom most of all there was reason to hate, because being a disciple, having shared the table and the salt, having seen the miracles and been deemed worthy of such great things, he acted more grievously than any, not stoning indeed, nor insulting Him, but betraying and giving Him up, observe in how friendly sort He receiveth this man, washing his feet; for even in this way He desired to restrain him from that wickedness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet it was in His power, had He willed it, to have withered him like the fig-tree, to have cut him in two as He rent the rocks, to have cleft him asunder like the veil; but He would not lead him away from his design by compulsion, but by choice.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore He washed his feet; and not even by this was that wretched and miserable man shamed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Before the feast of the Passover,” it saith, “Jesus knowing that His hour was come.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Not then “knowing,” but (it means) that He did what He did having “known” long ago.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “That He should depart.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Magnificently the Evangelist calleth His death, “departure.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Having loved His own, He loved them unto the end.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou how when about to leave them He showeth greater love?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the, “having loved, He loved them unto the end,” showeth that he omitted nothing of the things which it was likely that one who earnestly loved would do.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why, then did He not this from the beginning?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He worketh the greatest things last, so as to render more intense their attachment, and to lay up for them beforehand much comfort, against the terrible things that were about to fall on them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: St. John calls them “His own,” in respect of personal attachment, since he calls others also “His own,” in respect of the work of creation; as when he saith, “His own received Him not.” ( c. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But what meaneth, “which were in the world”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because the dead also were “His own,” Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and the men of that sort, but they were not in the world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou that He is the God both of the Old and New (Testament)?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But what meaneth, “He loved them unto the end”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It stands for, “He continued loving them unceasingly,” and this the Evangelist mentions as a sure proof of great affection.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Elsewhere indeed He spake of another (proof), the laying down life for His friends; but that had not yet come to pass.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And wherefore did He this thing “now”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because it was far more wonderful at a time when He appeared more glorious in the sight of all men.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Besides, He left them no small consolation now that He was about to depart, for since they were going to be greatly grieved, He by these means introduceth also comfort to the grief.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And supper being ended, the devil having now put it into the heart of Judas to betray Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This the Evangelist hath said amazed, showing that Jesus washed the man who had already chosen to betray Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This also proves his great wickedness, that not even the having shared the salt restrained him, (a thing which is most able to restrain wickedness;) not the fact that even up to the last day, his Master continued to bear with him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Jesus knowing that the Father had given all things into His hands, and that He was come from God, and went to God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Here the Evangelist saith, even wondering, that one so great, so very great, who came from God and went to Him, who ruleth over all, did this thing, and disdained not even so to undertake such an action.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And by the “giving over,” methinks St. John means the salvation of the faithful.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when He saith, “All things are given over to Me of My Father” ( Matt. xi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 27 ), He speaketh of this kind of giving over; as also in another place He saith, “Thine they were, and Thou gavest them Me” ( c. xvii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 6 ); and again, “No man can come unto Me except the Father draw him” ( c. vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 44 ); and, “Except it be given him from heaven.” ( c. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The Evangelist then either means this, or that Christ would be nothing lessened by this action, since He came from God, and went to God, and possessed all things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But when thou hearest of “giving over,” understand it in no human sense, for it showeth how He honoreth the Father, and His unanimity with Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For as the Father giveth over to Him, so He to the Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this Paul declares, saying, “When He shall have given over the kingdom to God, even the Father.” ( 1 Cor. xv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But St. John hath said it here in a more human sense, showing His great care for them, and declaring His unutterable love, that He now cared for them as for His own; teaching them the mother of all good, even humblemindedness, which He said was both the beginning and the end of virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And not without a reason is added the, “He came from God and went to God”: but that we may learn that He did what was worthy of One who came thence and went thither, trampling down all pride.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And having risen from supper, and laid aside His garments.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Observe how not by the washing only, but in another way also He exhibiteth humility.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For it was not before reclining, but after they had all sat down, then He arose.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In the next place, He doth not merely wash them, but doth so, putting off His garments.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And He did not even stop here, but girded Himself with a towel.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Nor was He satisfied with this, but Himself filled (the basin), and did not bid another fill it; He did all these things Himself, showing by all that we must do such things, when we are engaged in well doing, not merely for form’s sake, but with all zeal.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now He seemeth to me to have washed the feet of the traitor first from its saying,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He began to wash the disciples’ feet,” and adding,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Then cometh He to Simon Peter and Peter saith unto Him, Lord, dost Thou wash my feet?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “With those hands,” he saith, “with which Thou hast opened eyes, and cleansed lepers, and raised the dead?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this (question) is very emphatic; wherefore He needed not to have said any more than the, “Thou”; for even of itself this would have sufficed to convey the whole.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Some one might reasonably enquire, how none of the others forbade Him, but Peter only, which was a mark of no slight love and reverence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then is the cause?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He seemeth to me to have washed the traitor first, then to have come to Peter, and that the others were afterwards instructed from his case.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That He washed some one other before him is clear from its saying, “But when He came to Peter.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet the Evangelist is not a vehement accuser, for the “began,” is the expression of one implying this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And even if Peter were the first, yet it is probable that the traitor, being a forward person, had reclined even before the chief.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For by another circumstance also his forwardness is shown, when He dippeth with his Master in the dish, and being convicted, feels no compunction; while Peter being rebuked but once on a former occasion, and for words which he spake from loving affection, was so abashed, that being even distressed and trembling, he begged another to ask a question.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But Judas, though continually convicted, felt not. ( Ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When therefore He came to Peter, he saith unto Him, “Lord, dost Thou wash my feet?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He saith unto him, What I do thou knowest not now, but thou shalt know here after.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That is “thou shalt know how great is the gain from this, the profit of the lesson, and how it is able to guide us into all humblemindedness.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then doth Peter?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He still hinders Him, and saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Thou shalt never wash my feet.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “What doest thou, Peter?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Rememberest thou not those former words?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Saidst thou not, ‘Be merciful to Thyself,’ and heardest thou not in return, ‘Get thee behind Me, Satan’? ( Matt. xvi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Art thou not even so sobered, but art thou yet vehement?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Yea,” he saith, “for what is being done is a great matter, and full of amazement.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since then he did this from exceeding love, Christ in turn subdueth him by the same; and as there He effected this by sharply rebuking him, and saying, “Thou art an offense unto Me,” so here also by saying,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If I wash thee not, thou hast no part with Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then saith that hot and burning one?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Lord, not my feet only, but also my hands and my head.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Vehement in deprecation, he becometh yet more vehement in acquiescence; but both from love.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For why said He not wherefore He did this, instead of adding a threat?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because Peter would not have been persuaded.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For had He said, “Suffer it, for by this I persuade you to be humbleminded,” Peter would have promised it ten thousand times, in order that his Master might not do this thing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But now what saith He?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He speaketh of that which Peter most feared and dreaded, the being separated from Him; for it is he who continually asks, “Whither goest Thou?” ( Ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore also he said, “I will give even my life for Thee.” ( Ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if, after hearing, “What I do thou knowest not now, but thou shalt know hereafter,” he still persisted, much more would he have done so had he learnt (the meaning of the action).

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Therefore said He, “but thou shalt know hereafter,” as being aware, that should he learn it immediately he would still resist.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And Peter said not, “Tell me, that I may suffer Thee,” but (which was much more vehement) he did not even endure to learn, but withstands Him, saying, “Thou shalt never wash my feet.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But as soon as He threatened, he straightway relaxed his tone.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But what meaneth, “Thou shalt know after this”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “After this?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “When in My Name thou shalt have cast out devils; when thou shalt have seen Me taken up into Heaven, when thou shalt have learnt from the Spirit that I sit on His right hand, then shalt thou understand what is being done now.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then saith Christ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When Peter said, “not my feet only, but also my hands and my head,” He replieth,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “He that is washed, needeth not save to wash his feet, but is clean every whit; and ye are clean, but not all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He knew who should betray Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And if they are clean, why washeth He their feet?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That we may learn to be modest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On which account He came not to any other part of the body, but to that which is considered more dishonorable than the rest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But what is, “He that is washed”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It is instead of, “he that is clean.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Were they then clean, who had not yet been delivered from their sins, nor deemed worthy of the Spirit, since sin still had the mastery, the handwriting of the curse still remaining, the victim not having yet been offered?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How then calleth He them “clean”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That thou mayest not deem them clean, as delivered from their sins, He addeth, Behold, “ye are clean through the word that I have spoken unto you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That is, “In this way ye are so far clean; ye have received the light, ye have been freed from Jewish error.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the Prophet also saith, ‘Wash you, make you clean, put away the wickedness from your souls’ ( Isa. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 16 , LXX.); so that such a one is washed and is clean.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since then these men had cast away all wickedness from their souls, and had companied with Him with a pure mind, therefore He saith according to the word of the Prophet, “he that is washed is clean already.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For in that place also It meaneth not the “washing” of water, practiced by the Jews; but the cleansing of the conscience.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Be we then also clean; learn we to do well.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But what is “well”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Judge for the fatherless, plead for the widow; and come, let us reason together, saith the Lord.” ( Isa. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: There is frequent mention in the Scriptures of widows and orphans, but we make no account of this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet consider how great is the reward.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Though,” it saith, “your sins be as scarlet, I will whiten them as snow; though they be red like crimson, I will whiten them as wool.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For a widow is an unprotected being, therefore He taketh much care for her.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For they, when it is even in their power to contract a second marriage, endure the hardships of widowhood through fear of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then all, both men and women, stretch forth our hands to them, that we may never undergo the sorrows of widow-hood; or if we should have to undergo them, let us lay up a great store of kindness for ourselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Not small is the power of the widow’s tears, it is able to open heaven itself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us not then trample on them, nor make their calamity worse, but assist them by every means.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If so we do, we shall put around ourselves much safety, both in the present life, and in that which is to come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For not here alone, but there also will they be our defenders, cutting away most of our sins by reason of our beneficence towards them, and causing us to stand boldly before the judgment-seat of Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Which may it come to pass that we all obtain, through the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom be glory for ever and ever.
